mcqs.question: What does Newton's First Law of Motion state?
options: A. A body in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by an external force.
B. Force equals mass times acceleration.
C. Every action has an equal and opposite reaction.
D. Energy cannot be created or destroyed.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of Newton's First Law?
options: A. A ball rolling down a hill continues to roll.
B. A book resting on a table remains at rest.
C. A rocket launching into space.
D. A car accelerating on a highway.

mcqs.question: Inertia is defined as:
options: A. The tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion.
B. The force required to change an object's motion.
C. The mass of an object multiplied by the acceleration due to gravity.
D. The energy possessed by an object due to its motion.

mcqs.question: Which of the following will have more inertia?
options: A. A feather
B. A book
C. A car
D. A truck

mcqs.question: If no net external force acts on an object, it will:
options: A. Change its velocity.
B. Remain at rest or continue to move at a constant velocity.
C. Always come to a stop.
D. Move in a circular path.

fill_in_the_blanks.question: According to Newton's First Law, an object at rest will remain at rest unless acted upon by a __________ force.
The property of matter that resists changes in motion is called __________.
An object in motion will continue to move in a __________ line at a constant speed unless acted upon by a force.
The amount of inertia an object has depends on its __________.
Newton's First Law is also known as the law of __________.

short_questions.question: Give an example of Newton's First Law in daily life.
What happens to a moving object if no forces act on it?
Name the scientist who formulated the laws of motion.
